content.full_text:
SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL
P.O. BOX 94249
SACRAMENTO, CA 94249-0001
(916) 445-2363

DISTRICT OFFICE
8510 SOUTH BROADWAY
LOS ANGELES, CA 90003
(213) 751-1087

Assembly
California Legislature

COMMITTEES
BANKING, FINANCE AND
BONDED INDEBTEDNESS
ELECTIONS, REAPPORTIONMENT
AND CONSTITUTIONAL AMENDMENTS
HUMAN SERVICES
JUDICIARY
LABOR AND EMPLOYMENT

OCT 28 | 2015

MARGUERITE ARCHIE-HUDSON
ASSEMBLYWOMAN, FORTY-EIGHTH DISTRICT

October 23, 1991

Ms. Louisa Menchaca
FPPC
428 "J" Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Menchaca:

This letter is to memorialize our telephone conversation on
Tuesday, October 22, 1991 regarding employment prohibitions
pursuant to the Political Reform Act on legislative employees
while on leave without pay from the State of California Assembly.

As I explained to you in our conversation, as of November 1,
1991 I will be on leave without pay from my present position as
principal assistant to Assemblywoman Marguerite Archie-Hudson due
to Proposition 140 mandatory budget reduction cuts. However,
during the month of November, I intend to be in the employment of
a private brokerage firm in Chicago, Illinois. In connection with
said employment, I will be acting in the capacity of a consultant
to the firm. In particular, I will be offering my expert advice on
business opportunities in California and other states.

It is my understanding in speaking with you that there are no
restrictions pursuant to the Political Reform Act which would
prevent me from acting in the position as we discussed at length,
so long as, any income earned is reported on my annual FPPC
disclosure statement. In addition, I have taken the liberty of
contacting Al Gress, Assembly Office of Legislative Counsel, who
has also informed me that the above situation would not run afoul
of any conflict of interest rules within the jurisdiction of the
Legislature.

Thank you for your assistance with this matter.

Sincerely,

L. Kenneth Brooks
Principal Assistant

cc: Al Gress

Printed on Recycled Paper

sections.question_synthetic: Can a legislative employee on leave without pay from the California Assembly work as a consultant for a private brokerage firm in Chicago while on unpaid leave, and what are the reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence documenting a prior telephone conversation, not a formal FPPC advice letter with traditional Q/C sections. The letter paraphrases what the sender believes FPPC staff stated verbally but does not contain the FPPC's formal written response. No formal CONCLUSION section from FPPC is present in this document. Confidence is moderate due to unclear OCR in places and the informal nature of the documentation.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Can a legislative employee on leave without pay from the California Assembly work as a consultant for a private brokerage firm in Chicago while on unpaid leave, and what are the reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act?

embedding.summary: This is a letter from L. Kenneth Brooks, Principal Assistant to Assemblywoman Marguerite Archie-Hudson, to FPPC staff member Louisa Menchaca documenting a prior telephone conversation regarding employment prohibitions under the Political Reform Act for legislative employees on leave without pay. Brooks confirms he discussed his intention to work as a consultant for a private brokerage firm in Chicago during November 1991 while on unpaid leave due to Proposition 140 budget cuts, and states that Menchaca indicated no restrictions would apply as long as income is reported on annual FPPC disclosures.
